title: Model Version Tracking and Immutable Version IDs (Art. 13)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
index=ai_serving sourcetype="ai:gateway:access" earliest=-4h
| eval semver_ok=if(match(model_version,"^v?\d+\.\d+\.\d+"),1,0)
| stats count as reqs, sum(semver_ok) as semver_hits by deployment, model_version
| eval semver_pct=round(100*semver_hits/reqs,2)
| where isnull(model_version) OR model_version="" OR semver_ok=0
| sort - reqs
value: Detects production traffic served from non-semver or missing model_version to support traceability and change control evidence.
implementation: (1) Enforce semver in model registry webhook; (2) block deploy if version missing; (3) alert on null model_version > 10/min; (4) map deployment to ITSI entity.
visualization: Pie chart (semver_ok), Table (bad versions), Column chart (reqs by deployment)
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
